summary:
The choice remains justified due to the versatility and balance provided by suspension liners; other options have specific drawbacks that make them less ideal choices compared to this option which addresses multiple needs effectively without significant downsides noted clinically so far. Agent2 concluded based primarily on the broad applicability and minimal complications associated with using suspension liners over alternative materials available today within dentistry practice settings. To summarize:

Suspension liners are preferred for their versatile properties providing adequate thermal insulation while being easy to handle. Other materials may present limitations regarding compatibility or accessibility making suspensions more reliable overall according to current evidence-based practices.
